methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Manifold Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Gleb Kuznetsov and Pierce Ogden that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms in the biotech and health sector at the same or adjacent stage, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, implies $80M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $805M, with the peer set ranging from $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Manifold's revenue is not on file; the Biotech & Health sector median P/S of 6.6x cannot be deployed without this data point.

The two applicable methods produce a wide range. The triangulated valuation spans $68M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $442M. The wide dispersion reflects the stage gap between Manifold (Series A) and most peers (Series B+), creating substantial uncertainty around which comparables best represent future outcomes. Key unknowns include pipeline strength, capital requirements to next inflection, and whether the company's risk profile aligns with the lower or upper quartile of the peer set.
